incorrects.description: Student selected 2, confusing scale of 2 with scale of 5

params.description: Key on scale of 5 graph highlights.

steps.text: You're looking at the scale of 2 graph. Look at the key on the scale of 5 graph. What does it say each symbol equals?

incorrects.description: Student selected 10, possibly doubling the scale value

params.description: Key on scale of 5 graph highlights.

steps.text: The key tells you exactly what each symbol stands for. Read the key on the scale of 5 graph. What number do you see?

incorrects.description: Student selected 1, confusing scale with counting by 1s

params.description: Key on scale of 5 graph highlights.

steps.text: When the scale is 5, each symbol represents 5 items, not 1. Look at the key. It shows what each symbol equals.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Key highlights showing Each ⭐ = 5. One symbol pulses to demonstrate the connection.

steps.text: Let me show you. The key says Each star equals 5. That means every symbol you see on this graph represents 5 items. When the scale is 5, each symbol stands for 5. That's what makes it a scale of 5.
